findings.title: File Lock Timeout Fallback Proceeds Without Lock - Data Corruption Risk
findings.description: When FileLock.acquire() times out after 5 seconds, the code at lines 1070-1123 in handoff_store.py proceeds with the write operation WITHOUT the lock. This is a dangerous fallback that creates a race condition where multiple concurrent compactions can corrupt the task file.

evidence.proof: Calculation: 2 concurrent compactions both timeout after 5s then proceed without lock causing os.replace() race condition where last write wins and data is lost

impact.business_consequence: Concurrent terminal compactions will corrupt task_tracker JSON files causing silent data loss and inconsistent handoff state

recommendation.action: Remove the TimeoutError fallback. If lock acquisition fails either block retry with longer timeout or abort raise exception. Never proceed without lock.
recommendation.code_fix: Remove lines 1070-1123. Replace with: except TimeoutError: raise HandoffStoreError(f'Failed to acquire lock after {LOCK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s - concurrent compaction detected')
